Claim: Sensitivity guidance selectively conceals designated relation labels while retaining non-sensitive relation semantics.
Primary outcome: Original sensitive-label concealment; non-sensitive semantic retention
Comparator: relation_only vs random_relation_budget; full vs random_full_budget
Experimental scope: 5 datasets; 15 protection seeds; primary matrix; minority-prevalence stress test reported separately
Inferential unit: Paired protection-seed values within dataset and metric
Multiplicity correction: Holm within the two pre-specified matched-control comparisons for each dataset-metric family
Evidence location: Section 6.1; Table 2; Figure 4; Supplementary Tables S10 and S27

Claim: Designated degree-preserving variants preserve every node degree exactly.
Primary outcome: Changed-degree-node count; degree-sequence L1 error; exact per-node degree-preservation indicator
Comparator: Degree-preserving variants; random_edge_dropout as a non-degree-preserving reference; 2K for stronger structural constraint
Experimental scope: 5 datasets; 15 protection seeds; 450 designated degree-preserving rows plus 2K control records
Multiplicity correction: Not applicable to the exact invariant; comparative structural outcomes use their protocol-specific families
Evidence location: Section 6.2; Table 2; Supplementary Tables S6 and S17

Claim: Direct sensitive-label recovery can be eliminated while original-edge existence remains inferable.
Primary outcome: Original relation-label recovery; local structural AUC; hybrid edge-inference AUC
Comparator: Baseline and evaluated transformation variants by sensitive/non-sensitive target group
Experimental scope: 5 datasets; 15 protection seeds; 1,050 condition summaries; 210,000 candidate records
Inferential unit: Condition summaries over protection seeds; attack outcomes interpreted separately by feature set
Multiplicity correction: Descriptive fixed-edge outcomes in the main text; protocol-specific audit details in supplementary evidence
Evidence location: Section 6.3; Table 4; Figure 6A-B; fixed-edge supplementary records

Claim: Structure-only and representation-based attack outcomes are dataset- and attacker-dependent.
Comparator: full vs baseline and matched controls
Experimental scope: PubMed and SynthPII; structure-only, spectral, and released-view GCN-GAE protocols
Inferential unit: Protection-seed summaries; spectral and GCN-GAE split results averaged or hierarchically resampled as specified in Section 5.6
Multiplicity correction: Structure-only comparator family; spectral global and metric-level Holm over 48 tests; GCN-GAE Holm over 36 comparisons
Evidence location: Sections 5.4, 5.6, and 6.3; Tables 5-6; Supplementary Tables S22-S23 and S28-S29

Claim: The default protected marker does not conceal supplied-policy membership; coarse covers trade leakage for semantic retention.
Comparator: Default marker; omission; 10%, 25%, 50%, 75%, and 100% shared-cover settings
Multiplicity correction: Holm separately within each reported metric; exact sign-flip and bootstrap diagnostics as specified
Evidence location: Section 6.3; Supplementary Table S20; Discussion Section 7.5

Claim: The transformation faithfully executes the supplied policy but does not correct policy errors.
Comparator: Clean policy vs false-negative, false-positive, and symmetric error conditions
Experimental scope: 5 datasets; 15 protection seeds; error rates 5%, 10%, and 20%
Inferential unit: Paired protection-seed values within metric and error family
Multiplicity correction: Holm separately within each reported metric

Claim: Sensitivity guidance does not generally maximize structural overlap.
Primary outcome: Path-support retention; complete-path preservation; endpoint reachability; relevant-node and relevant-edge recall
Comparator: full vs baseline, random_edge_dropout, and random_full_budget
Experimental scope: 5 datasets; fixed retrieval paths and relevant-subgraph protocol; 15 protection seeds
Inferential unit: Paired protection-seed values within dataset and metric
Multiplicity correction: Holm across six full-versus-comparator tests within each dataset-metric family

Claim: Under predefined structured relation-sequence QA, TAFP retains non-sensitive answering utility while reducing sensitive-relation disclosure relative to sensitivity-blind controls.
Experimental scope: 5 datasets; 718 fixed queries; 15 protection seeds; 4 graph conditions; 43,080 generations
Inferential unit: Query values aggregated within dataset/seed/task, then dataset-level values averaged within each protection seed; 15 matched seed means
Multiplicity correction: Holm within each scope-task-metric family across the three comparators
Evidence location: Sections 5.5, 5.6, and 6.5; Table 9; Figure 7; Supplementary Tables S24-S26
